new_classes.definition_en: Spirits not elsewhere specified or included in CN heading 2208, including aniseed spirits, raki, agave spirits other than tequila (e.g. mezcal), spirits distilled from aromatic herbs, digestive bitters, aquavit, kranawitter, spirits distilled from roots (e.g. gentian spirits), sorghum spirits, Hefebrand (lees spirit), classified under CN subheading 2208 90 56 (Chapter 22).

new_classes.label_en: Herb, Root and Aroma Distillation Process
new_classes.definition_en: Process of distilling fermented or macerated materials derived from aromatic herbs, roots, seeds (e.g. aniseed, gentian), agave plants (other than blue agave used for tequila), or grain-based lees (Hefebrand), to produce spirits classified under CN subheading 2208 90 56.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the CN code 22089056 is bound via codeString hasValue; (2) a new OtherSpirit class is introduced as a subclass of eucn:Spirit, scoped to spirits not covered by any named sibling subheading (aniseed spirits, raki, mezcal-type agave spirits, herb spirits, digestive bitters, aquavit, kranawitter, root spirits, sorghum spirits, Hefebrand/lees spirit); (3) a new HerbRootAromaDistillation process class is introduced to represent the production process characteristic of this residual group. Disjointness from sibling Spirit subclasses is expressed via complement restrictions on producedBy. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the full enumeration of spirit types (aniseed, raki, mezcal, aquavit, etc.) is expressed only in the definition text rather than as explicit named sub-classes or property value sets — this would require additional classes or individuals for each named spirit type, which is deliberately withheld to avoid over-specification; (b) the 'other than tequila' exclusion for agave spirits cannot be fully axiomatised without a Tequila class being present in the TBox.
